title: Arrangement for testing electric car components
abstract: This document describes an arrangement for testing electric car components. The arrangement comprises a transportable container (100) housing at least a test chamber (102) for testing the electric car components; a climate control system (110) for controlling climate conditions in the test chamber of the container; and means for testing the electric car components in the test chamber of the container.
claims: Claims Claims 1. An arrangement for testing electric components of a car, comprising: a transportable container housing at least a test chamber for testing the electric components; a climate control system for controlling climate conditions in the test chamber of the container; and means for testing the electric components in the test chamber of the container. 2. The arrangement of claim 1, the container comprising a fireproof inner wall separating an interior of the container into the test chamber and a facility room. 3. The arrangement of claim 2, wherein all electric connections for testing the electric car components are arranged to and from the test chamber through the inner wall. 4. The arrangement of claim 2 or 3, wherein the container comprises an electric interface to electric input/output equipment configured to input electric inputs to an electric component under test in the test chamber, wherein the electric interface is provided as a connector or a fireproof passthrough in the inner wall. 5. The arrangement of claim 4, wherein the electric components comprise high-voltage batteries of electric cars, the arrangement further comprising the electric input/output equipment in the facility room and configured to input charge/discharge cycles to a battery under test. 6. The arrangement of any preceding claim, wherein a first unit of the climate control system is comprised in the test chamber and a second unit of the climate control system is provided outside the test chamber, and a coupling is provided between the first unit and the second unit. 7. The arrangement of claim 6, further comprising a platform constructed on a roof of the transportable container, and wherein the second unit of the climate control system is mounted or mountable on the platform. 8. The arrangement of claim 7, further comprising a roof interface between an interior of the container and the roof of the container, wherein the roof interface provides couplings between the first unit of the climate control system and the second unit of the climate control system. 9. The arrangement of any preceding claim, further comprising a test control system disposed in the facility room and configured to control the climate control system and the means for testing according to one or more testing protocols. 10. The arrangement of any preceding claim, wherein the container further comprises an electric interface disposed in an exterior wall of the container, the electric interface suitable for testing an electric car component outside the test chamber. 11. The arrangement of any preceding claim, further comprising: at least one door providing access to the test chamber from exterior of the container, wherein the at least one door contacts a door frame of the container through an insulator, and means for heating a contact surface at the location of the insulator. 12. The arrangement of any preceding claim, further comprising a further container arranged in parallel with the container, wherein the containers are secured to each other via locking means, and wherein at least some of side walls of the containers that face each other are removed to provide enlarged test chamber extending at least partially over both containers. 13. The arrangement of any preceding claim, wherein at least one inlet/outlet of a safety system is provided from the test chamber directly to an exterior of the container. 14. The arrangement of claim 13, further comprising a platform constructed on a roof of the transportable container, wherein the at least one inlet/outlet comprises a rupture panel on the roof of the container, and wherein the rupture panel is covered by one or more elements of the platform.
